chapter_title: Doctors in Secondary Schools – School program lead funding (Reference 64)
snippet: The Doctors in Secondary Schools Program (DiSS) External Link funds general practitioners (GPs) and practice nurses to attend 100 Victorian government secondary schools up to one day a week to provide medical advice and health care for those students most in need. Schools are required to appoint a Leading Teacher to hold overall responsibility for program coordination duties. Schools will be provided with funding equivalent to a 0.2 FTE at a Leading Teacher Level 3.1 pay rate to support program co-ordination responsibilities for the school year. A schools’ program co-ordination responsibilities can also rest with an Assistant Principal, given that it is more appropriate than appointing a Leading teacher. However, if a school appoints an Assistant Principal to the School Program Lead role, the school will still receive the same amount of funding as a Leading Teacher. Schools with the following campus types are eligible for DiSS Funding at school level.
